criteria.name: Strong Opening Hook
criteria.description: The first 50-150 characters (or 1-2 lines) must grab attention, stopping the scroll within 2 seconds. Use specific numbers, surprising observations, thought-provoking questions, or a bold statement. For maximum impact, aim for under 8 words in the very first sentence.

criteria.name: Mobile-First Formatting
criteria.description: Structure your content for easy readability on mobile devices. Use short paragraphs (1-3 sentences), strategic line breaks, and ample white space to avoid a dense 'wall of text'.

criteria.name: Strategic Use of Visuals
criteria.description: Incorporate high-quality images, videos, infographics, or carousels (PDF documents) to increase engagement. Videos can get 5x more engagement, and carousels average high engagement rates. Upload natively to LinkedIn when possible.

criteria.name: Clear Call-to-Action (CTA)
criteria.description: Include a clear, concise, and actionable call-to-action, typically at the end of the post. Use strong action verbs (e.g., 'Learn more', 'Comment below', 'Download now') and create a sense of urgency. Aim for 2-5 words for a button CTA.

criteria.name: External Links in First Comment
criteria.description: Avoid placing external links directly in your main post body, as the LinkedIn algorithm often deprioritizes such content to keep users on the platform. Instead, share links in the first comment.

criteria.name: Value-Driven Content & Storytelling
criteria.description: Share insightful, valuable content that offers advice, insights, frameworks, how-tos, or resources. Employ a storytelling approach, sharing personal anecdotes, transformation stories, or case studies that resonate with your audience and make them feel smart for finding it.

criteria.name: Encourage Engagement with Questions/Polls
criteria.description: Actively invite participation by ending posts with genuine, open-ended questions or using LinkedIn polls to spark conversations. Respond to comments promptly to keep the discussion going, as early engagement signals value to the algorithm.

criteria.name: Authenticity & Human Touch
criteria.description: Ensure your posts reflect authenticity and a human touch. Share personal opinions and feelings to spark deeper conversations. Avoid content that feels overly corporate, preachy, or excessively AI-generated, as authentic content receives higher engagement.

criteria.name: Optimal Post Length (Characters/Words)
criteria.description: For general posts, aim for 150-300 characters for quick tips or around 900-1200 characters (200-400 words) for deeper dives. Longer, valuable content (1900-2000 words, often in articles) can also perform well for reach and authority building. Short, punchy posts of ~25 words or fewer are also effective.

criteria.name: Strategic Hashtag Use
criteria.description: Use 2-3 (maximum 3-5) relevant, niche-specific hashtags at the end of your post to increase discoverability. Avoid overusing hashtags, as it can make your post appear spammy.

criteria.name: Tagging Relevant Connections
criteria.description: When appropriate, tag relevant individuals, companies, or influencers using the '@' symbol to increase visibility among their networks and invite their contribution to the conversation.

criteria.name: Professionalism & Relevance
criteria.description: Keep content professional and relevant to business and industry. While a human touch is important, avoid sharing overly personal information or content that would be more suitable for platforms like Facebook. Maintain credibility and trust.

criteria.name: Consistent Posting Schedule & Early Engagement
criteria.description: Post consistently (e.g., 3-5 times a week) without overdoing it (maintain ~12 hours between posts). The first 30-60 minutes after posting are critical for engagement signals, so be ready to interact with early comments.

criteria.name: Avoid Jargon and Clichés
criteria.description: Write in clear, simple language, avoiding excessive business jargon or clichés. This makes your content more accessible and relatable to a wider audience.

criteria.name: Use Bold/Italics Sparingly for Emphasis
criteria.description: Utilize bold and italics to highlight key points, statistics, or calls-to-action, but do so sparingly to avoid cluttering the post and maintain a professional appearance.
